title: Universal model-tier envelope floor: 73/91 profiles have NO envelope — the clamp cannot floor them (compiled default min=balanced)
description:
FOLLOW-UP to cycle-462's HIGH audit defect + cycle-463's partial fix (7216c726). Cycle 463 shipped the clamp-up floor (model_routing_clamp.go: tier below envelope min clamps UP with a recorded rejection) and guardrail projection into the advisor prompt — but BOTH only bind when prof.ModelTierEnvelope != nil, and 73 of 91 profiles have no envelope (verified 2026-07-02: security-scan, cache-strategy-scan, risk-register, contract-fuzz-probe... incl. security-gate phases). An advisor tier:fast proposal for those phases still dispatches unclamped. CURRENT MITIGATION: the operator catalog maps fast->sonnet, so the worst reachable downgrade is sonnet (haiku unreachable) — risk is contained but the structural hole remains.

FIX (single-source, NO 91-file churn): a COMPILED DEFAULT envelope in the clamp/policy resolution — absent ModelTierEnvelope resolves to {min: balanced, max: deep} (per the operator low-model rule: low tiers ONLY for an explicit mechanical whitelist). Profiles that legitimately want fast declare an explicit envelope {min: fast} — the whitelist is the EXPLICIT opt-in (doc-sync, changelog-sync, locale-format-check, close-checklist, runbook-draft, telemetry-coverage-check, dependency-map, scope-baseline, behavior-baseline, flake-rerun-scan). Follow the compiled-default-with-config-override pattern (internal/policy precedent: observer/swarm defaults).

TDD: red test proving an envelope-less profile clamps a fast proposal UP to balanced with a recorded rejection; whitelist profile with explicit {min:fast} accepts fast; golden matrix extended (model_routing_clamp_matrix_test.go). apicover -enforce + go test -race clean. ALSO: sweep the 10 mechanical-whitelist profiles to declare their explicit {min:fast} envelopes in the same slice.
